pi_items.source: setup_task.sh explicitly writes the mission specification document to this path.
export_result.sh explicitly parses this exact file path to extract delta-V values.
setup_task.sh generates the mission specification with SMA = 6556.14 km, which is also checked by verifier.py via regex.
setup_task.sh generates the mission specification with Inclination = 28.5 deg, which is also checked by verifier.py via regex.
verifier.py checks if TLI delta-V is greater than or equal to this fallback minimum for scoring.
verifier.py checks if TLI delta-V is less than or equal to this fallback maximum for scoring.
verifier.py checks if LOI delta-V is greater than or equal to this fallback minimum for scoring.
verifier.py checks if LOI delta-V is less than or equal to this fallback maximum for scoring.
verifier.py loads this bound as an acceptable minimum for the total Delta-V (though unused in scoring).
verifier.py loads this bound as an acceptable maximum for the total Delta-V (though unused in scoring).
verifier.py checks if transfer time is greater than or equal to this minimum limit for scoring.
verifier.py checks if transfer time is less than or equal to this maximum limit for scoring.
verifier.py loads this bound as an acceptable minimum lunar altitude (though unused in scoring).
verifier.py loads this bound as an acceptable maximum lunar altitude (though unused in scoring).

privileged_info_summary: Verified that the lunar mission task is fully synthetic. The setup script explicitly generates the mission specification document at '/home/ga/Desktop/lunar_mission_spec.txt' defining an initial LEO SMA of 6556.14 km and inclination of 28.5 degrees. The export script extracts metrics exactly from '/home/ga/GMAT_output/lunar_transfer_results.txt'. The verifier directly validates TLI Delta-V between 2800 and 3500 m/s, LOI Delta-V between 500 and 1200 m/s, and transfer time between 2 and 7 days. Total Delta-V and Lunar Altitude bounds are loaded by the verifier but not checked in the scoring logic.
